finding: Slice 1 and Slice 3 are structurally coupled: settings-agents.tsx (Slice 1, new) is imported by dialog-settings.tsx (Slice 3, modify). If Slice 3 is implemented before Slice 1, the Agents tab import will reference a non-existent component. The plan must specify the inter-slice dependency contract (interface/export shape of settings-agents.tsx) or merge these slices into one.
repair_path: Add an explicit dependency declaration: Slice 1 must be implemented first (or simultaneously with Slice 3). Document the expected exports from settings-agents.tsx (component name, props interface) so Slice 1 and Slice 3 can proceed in parallel without deadlocking.

finding: The plan proposes adding `getCustomAgents`/`setCustomAgents` to ElectronAPI (preload/types.ts) and dedicated IPC handlers in ipc.ts. However, the codebase already has generic `storeGet`/`storeSet`/`storeDelete` IPC handlers that accept a `name` (store name) parameter — these already work with any named electron-store instance. Using `name="desktop-custom-agents"` with the existing store handlers requires ZERO new IPC handlers and ZERO new ElectronAPI properties. The plan's proposed IPC approach creates unnecessary surface area expansion.
repair_path: Remove dedicated IPC handler creation from the plan. The agent config UI should use existing `window.electronAPI.storeGet("desktop-custom-agents", key)` and `window.electronAPI.storeSet("desktop-custom-agents", key, value)` calls. Only the store `name` constant needs to be defined. This eliminates all proposed changes to ipc.ts and preload/types.ts for this feature.

finding: The plan claims `settings.agents.title` and `settings.agents.description` i18n keys need to be added to en.ts. These keys ALREADY EXIST in ALL 18 translation files (en.ts line 908-909, plus all 17 locale files). The plan's file table incorrectly lists this as work. Adding duplicate keys would create a runtime override at best and a build error at worst.
repair_path: Remove the i18n modification from the plan for the agents title/description. If the plan needs additional agent-specific i18n keys (e.g., form field labels, button text), specify the NEW key names explicitly instead of vaguely mentioning "agent config UI i18n keys."

finding: Compaction indicator badge (Slice 2) is underspecified. The plan says "Check if session events indicate compaction recently occurred" but there is NO existing compaction data source in the codebase — a grep for "compact" across packages/app/src returns zero results. No session event type for compaction exists. This feature cannot be implemented without either (a) discovering a compaction event source from the SDK/server API, or (b) defining one. The plan gives no guidance on where compaction events come from.
repair_path: Specify the compaction event source: either (a) check if the server sends compaction events via the sync protocol (investigate SDK session events), (b) derive compaction from metrics state (e.g., if usage% dropped >10% between consecutive measurements), or (c) defer this feature and clearly document it as blocked on server-side compaction events being plumbed to the client.

finding: The plan only mentions modifying en.ts for i18n, but the codebase has 18 translation files (en, ar, br, bs, da, de, es, fr, ja, ko, no, pl, ru, uk, th, tr, zh, zht). The existing i18n parity test (parity.test.ts) only checks 2 specific keys, but other tests or the build may assume locale consistency. If adding new i18n keys, all 18 locales must be updated. The plan's omission of this scope is a risk.
repair_path: Either (a) explicitly state that new i18n keys will be added to ALL 18 locale files, (b) reference the `parity.test.ts` to confirm the test only checks the 2 keys and won't fail on missing new keys, or (c) use a fallback mechanism in the i18n system.

finding: ProgressCircleProps currently has no color/warning state: only `percentage`, `size?`, `strokeWidth?`, and `class`/`classList`. The plan suggests either "CSS-based via data-component/data-slot selectors" or "adding a warning/critical prop." These are very different approaches with different coupling and surface area implications. Adding a prop couples the UI library to app-level semantics (context pressure). The CSS approach is cleaner but requires verifying the data-component/data-slot pattern works for ProgressCircle (it's an SVG element).
repair_path: Specify which approach to use with rationale. Recommend the CSS-only approach: pass warning/critical classes via existing `classList` prop on ProgressCircle and add CSS rules scoped to `[data-component="progress-circle"][data-slot="track"]` or similar. This avoids coupling the UI primitive to app concepts. Verify the progress-circle SVG renders with data attributes before committing to this path.

finding: The plan's dirty file preservation section mentions only additive changes to ipc.ts, preload/types.ts, dialog-settings.tsx, and en.ts. However, BOTH ipc.ts AND preload/types.ts are ALREADY dirty from concurrent sessions with non-trivial unstaged diffs: ipc.ts has install-cli handler added, types.ts has getDesktopPluginConfig added. Multiple agents editing the same dirty files increases merge conflict risk. The plan does not address this coordination risk.
repair_path: Add an explicit deconfliction step: (1) Check current git status of these files before editing, (2) Use `search_replace` with exact context matching rather than positional insertion, (3) Verify no concurrent edits to the same files by other sessions before committing. Consider whether the generic storeGet/storeSet approach (see blocking finding #2) eliminates the need to touch ipc.ts and preload/types.ts entirely.

finding: The plan lists verification steps at the end but they are incomplete and untestable as stated. Step 2 "Verify IPC handlers registered" has no runtime test. Step 4 "Verify context indicator shows compaction events" has no test because compaction events source is undefined. Step 6 "Verify keyboard navigation works" requires manual testing. The plan needs concrete, automated verification steps — at minimum: (a) `cd packages/app && bun run typecheck`, (b) existing test suite passes, (c) type-level verification that ElectronAPI contracts are satisfied.
repair_path: Add specific, executable verification steps: (1) `cd packages/app && bun run typecheck && bun run test` — full typecheck + test suite, (2) "Verify session-context-metrics.test.ts still passes when usage% crosses 80% threshold" (there IS an existing test for this), (3) "Verify all 3 features can be enabled/disabled without crash" — this is a manual acceptance step. Remove untestable steps like "compaction event" without a data source.

finding: The agent edit form specifies "Model (select from connected providers' models)" but the plan doesn't address the timing dependency: available models are fetched asynchronously via the server. The dialog needs a loading state while models are loading, and a disabled/no-models-available state if providers haven't connected. This is a missing edge case in the plan.
repair_path: Add the model-select loading/empty states to the agent edit dialog specification. The Model select should show "Loading models..." while providers load, and "No models available" if no providers are connected.

finding: 4 blocking falsifiers: saveCustomAgents has no error handling (silent data loss), generic store bypass corrupts typed namespaces, zero runtime validation on IPC boundary, duplicate AgentDef type will diverge.
repair_path: See 4 JSON repair directives in combined review verdict. Key: async saveCustomAgents with try/catch + revert, reserved store name blocklist, Array.isArray + AgentDef shape validation, unify AgentDef to shared source.
